title: pectocarya platycarpa
intro: Pectocarya platycarpa, also known as needle-and-spur gilia, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its unique needle-like leaves and small, blue-purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pectocarya platycarpa include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Pectocarya platycarpa include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
